color_profiles.name: Robin's Egg Blue
color_profiles.cause: High Cu2+ content; minimal Fe substitution for Al.

color_profiles.name: Matrix Turquoise
color_profiles.cause: Host rock (limonite, quartz, pyrite, iron oxides) veining through turquoise mass.

color_profiles.name: Green Turquoise
color_profiles.cause: Higher Fe3+ substituting for Al3+; or Zn or V substitution.

visual_notes: Turquoise is one of the most culturally loaded minerals in human history. The blue that defined a color name ('turquoise'), ancient trade routes, and sacred objects across the Old and New Worlds. Matrix spiderweb patterns are prized for their aesthetic complexity — black or brown veins threading through blue-green backgrounds.

optical_effects.fluorescence: Typically none to faint; some specimens show weak blue-green or yellow under LW UV
optical_effects.chatoyancy: Rare; fibrous material can show weak cat's eye
